sourceMovie.title: Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return works as a documentary movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Game of Thrones: The Last Watch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The documentary appeal of Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return — that tense edge, the craft — translates cleanly into Game of Thrones: The Last Watch (2019). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Pixar Story
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Pixar Story (2007) clearly understood what makes documentary work — the same quality that keeps Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Making Squid Game: The Challenge
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Making Squid Game: The Challenge (2023) is the kind of film Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The documentary overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Revisited
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Matrix Revisited shares Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return's gift for documentary — the 2001 film delivers momentum without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Five Obstructions
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return compelling — the documentary, the atmosphere — shows up in The Five Obstructions (2003) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Never Sleep Again: The Elm Street Legacy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The documentary energy of Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return is alive in Never Sleep Again: The Elm Street Legacy (2010), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: The Walking Dead: The Return
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Walking Dead: The Return (2024) operates in the same documentary as Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Irishman: In Conversation
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Irishman: In Conversation (2019) belongs to the same conversation as Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return — both anchor their documentary in authentic emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Capturing Avatar
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return's documentary stayed with you, Capturing Avatar (2010) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The tense through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Avatar: The Deep Dive - A Special Edition of 20/20
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return excels in documentary, Avatar: The Deep Dive - A Special Edition of 20/20 (2022) doubles down on craft to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

faq.question: What should I watch after Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return?
faq.answer: Try Game of Thrones: The Last Watch, The Pixar Story, Making Squid Game: The Challenge—each shares DNA with Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return?
faq.answer: Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return (2022) is best described as Documentary. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this documentary lane.
